steps.title: Identify the comparison structure
steps.content: The passage opens with “Like this alabaster box... is my heart,” using a simile to compare one physical element to one abstract element. The following lines extend this comparison, applying identical descriptors (frail, carved with dreams and exquisite thought) to both.

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A identifies exactly this function—the two underlined portions are the subjects (one physical, one abstract) of a comparison that extends throughout the text.

The other three choices misread the passage by imposing contrasts, decisions, or changes that the text does not contain.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
